metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4987
metadata.comment: Rationale: Figure 6c,d: Immunogold EM imaging of mouse cerebellum showed particles for Cbln3 were exclusively detected in the synaptic cleft between parallel fiber synaptic terminals.. Experimental description: M&M: "For production of a polyclonal antibody to Cbln3, cDNA fragment, which is preceded with a BamHI site and encodes 41-74 amino acid residues (NM198114; putative cerebellin-3 peptide for 59-74 and its preceding sequence for 41-58), was obtained by PCR. After the TA cloning using a pGEM-T Easy Vector System I kit (Promega, Madison, WI, USA), cDNA fragments were sequenced and excised by BamHI and EcoRI. These fragments were subcloned into Bam- HI / EcoRI site of pGEX4T-2 plasmid (GE Healthcare Bio-Sciences) for expression of glutathione S-transferase (GST) fusion proteins. GST fusion proteins were purified using glutathione-Sepharose 4B (GE Healthcare Bio-Sciences), according to the manufacturer's instruc- tions, and used for immunization to New Zealand White rabbits. Antigens were emulsified with Freund's complete or incomplete adjuvant (DIFCO, Detroit, MI, USA) and injected subcutaneously at intervals of 2 weeks. After the sixth injection, immunoglobulins specific to antigens were affinity-purified using synthetic peptide of 41-58 amino acid residues coupled to CNBr-activated Sepharose 4B (GE Healthcare Bio-Sciences).
